sources.citation: UN Human Rights/Panel of Experts: Wagner mining 2021

2022.assessment: Africa Corps (Wagner successor). Extractive revenues to Russia. Constitutional referendum. No EITI. No disclosure, opaque.

sources.citation: UN Panel of Experts: Africa Corps extractives 2022

2023.assessment: Africa Corps fully operational. Wagner → Africa Corps seamless transition for extractives. No EITI. No disclosure, opaque.

sources.citation: UN Panel of Experts: Africa Corps 2023

2024.assessment: Africa Corps extractives continuing. No EITI. No disclosure, opaque.

2025.assessment: March 2025: Africa Corps. No EITI. Extractive revenues opaque. No disclosure, opaque.

2000.assessment: No WJP data. French civil law system in theory. Government decrees dominant governing tool. Laws not systematically published or accessible. Courts functioning minimally in Bangui only. No accessible legal framework for most citizens. Minimal rule by decree.

2001.assessment: Coup attempt disrupted any rule of law. Emergency decrees. Courts non-functional during crisis. Minimal rule by decree.

2002.assessment: Bozizé insurgency. Legal framework non-existent in conflict zones. Bangui courts nominally operating. Minimal rule by decree.

2003.assessment: Bozizé coup March 2003. Constitution suspended. National Assembly dissolved. Courts closed briefly. Rule purely by decree from Conseil National de Transition. No accessible legal framework.

2004.assessment: Transitional government. Courts nominally reopened. Some legal framework restored. But laws not published, access to courts extremely limited. Minimal rule by decree.

2005.assessment: Bozizé elected. New constitution 2005. Courts nominally functioning. Laws not systematically published or accessible publicly. Minimal rule by decree.

2006.assessment: UFDR conflict. Legal framework non-existent in conflict areas. Bangui courts operating nominally. Difficult arbitrary enforcement.

2007.assessment: Birao peace. Bush War. Courts in Bangui nominal. Difficult arbitrary enforcement.

2008.assessment: Libreville peace. Some legal framework nominally published. Difficult arbitrary enforcement.

2009.assessment: HIPC completion. Some donor-required legal framework improvements. Courts functioning nominally in Bangui. Difficult arbitrary enforcement.

2010.assessment: Bozizé relatively stable. Courts nominal in Bangui. Laws published in official gazette but not accessible. Difficult arbitrary enforcement.

2011.assessment: Bozizé elections. Courts nominal. Pre-Séléka best legal framework period. Difficult arbitrary enforcement.

2012.assessment: Séléka forming. Legal framework deteriorating. Difficult arbitrary enforcement.

2013.assessment: Séléka coup. Constitution suspended. Courts closed. Djotodia ruling by decree. Multiple armed groups replacing any legal framework. No accessible legal framework.

2014.assessment: Civil war. Transitional government (Samba-Panza). Courts closed throughout country except minimal Bangui function. Armed group law in territories. No accessible framework.

2015.assessment: WJP 0.27/1.00 (27/100). Bangui Forum. New constitution passed. Some formal legal framework nominally restored. Courts functioning in Bangui only. No access outside Bangui. Minimal rule by decree in government-controlled areas.

sources.citation: WJP Rule of Law Index: CAR 2015 — 0.27/1.00

2016.assessment: WJP ~0.28/1.00. Touadéra elected. Some legal framework improvement. Courts nominally functioning in Bangui. Armed groups controlling 70%+ of territory — no law. Difficult arbitrary enforcement in government areas.

2017.assessment: WJP ~0.27/1.00. Worst violence since 2014. Legal system non-functional outside Bangui. Armed group law prevailing. Difficult arbitrary enforcement.

2018.assessment: WJP ~0.27. Wagner entering — parallel governance replacing formal legal framework in areas under Wagner control. Difficult arbitrary enforcement in Bangui; minimal/no framework elsewhere.

2019.assessment: APRC. WJP ~0.27. Armed group ministers — further blurring of legal framework. Difficult arbitrary enforcement.

2020.assessment: WJP ~0.26. CPC offensive. Legal framework deteriorating with war re-escalation. Difficult arbitrary enforcement.

2021.assessment: WJP ~0.27. Wagner defeated CPC. Touadéra reelected. Opposition leaders suppressed — legal framework used against critics. Difficult arbitrary enforcement.

2022.assessment: WJP ~0.27. Constitutional referendum pushed through. Africa Corps parallel governance. Difficult arbitrary enforcement.

2023.assessment: WJP ~0.27. Africa Corps entrenched. Constitutional referendum removed term limits. Legal system used to suppress opposition. Difficult arbitrary enforcement.

2024.assessment: Africa Corps. Elections approaching. Legal system used against opposition. Difficult arbitrary enforcement.

2025.assessment: March 2025: Africa Corps. Elections. Legal system used against critics. Difficult arbitrary enforcement.

2000.assessment: Patassé era. Some independent radio and print media in Bangui. Government-controlled radio dominant. Journalists covering sensitive topics (coup attempts, government corruption) harassed. Serious restrictions but some space. Very serious.

2001.assessment: Coup attempt May 2001 — journalists covering conflict harassed or self-censoring. Government accused media of destabilization. Very serious.

2002.assessment: Bozizé insurgency. Journalists covering northern conflict physically at risk. Bangui media somewhat functioning. Very serious.

2003.assessment: Bozizé coup March 2003. Independent media in Bangui harassed. Some journalists arrested briefly. Government took control of state radio. But overall not complete press freedom collapse — some independent radio continued. Very serious.

2004.assessment: Transitional. Some independent media returned. Bozizé seeking international legitimacy — some tolerance for press. Very serious but slight improvement from coup restrictions.

2005.assessment: Bozizé elected with elections. International scrutiny brought some media tolerance. Some private radio in Bangui. Journalists covering opposition sensitive. Very serious.

2006.assessment: UFDR conflict — journalists covering north at physical risk. Bangui media functioning with restrictions. Very serious.

2007.assessment: Bush War. Journalists covering conflict zones at risk. Birao peace deal — some improvement. Bangui independent media. Very serious.

2008.assessment: RSF ranked CAR ~100/173 (2008) — middle-lower of global rankings. Libreville peace. Some improvement. Difficult environment but not worst.

2009.assessment: LRA invasion eastern CAR. Journalists covering LRA activities at risk. Bangui media functioning. Very serious in conflict zones; difficult overall.

2010.assessment: Relative stability in Bangui. Private radio functioning. Some independent newspapers. Very serious — self-censorship on government criticism.

2011.assessment: RSF ~109/179. Pre-Séléka period. Bozizé re-elected. Some private media in Bangui. Journalists covering elections had space. Difficult environment.

2012.assessment: Séléka forming December 2012. Pre-crisis media relatively functioning. Journalists covering Séléka advance harassed. Very serious.

2013.assessment: Séléka coup March 2013. Journalists covering coup targeted. International journalists expelled briefly. State radio seized. Most outlets closed in Bangui. No press freedom during coup and immediate aftermath. Some limited reopening by year end under Djotodia.

2014.assessment: Civil war. Journalists covering conflict face death threats from both sides. CPJ documented journalist killings. Bangui some limited independent media under MINUSCA protection. No press freedom in conflict areas.

2015.assessment: Bangui Forum. Some improvement — Bangui media partially recovering. Radio Ndeke Luka (independent) operating. Journalists outside Bangui still extremely dangerous. Very serious.

2016.assessment: Touadéra elected. Some improvement in Bangui media environment. Independent radio functioning. Armed groups controlling 70%+ of territory with journalists excluded. Very serious.

2017.assessment: Worst violence since 2014 — journalists covering conflict at extreme risk. Bangui media somewhat functioning. RSF ~113/180. Very serious.

2018.assessment: Wagner entering. Journalists covering Wagner activities face intimidation and violence. State media increasingly pro-Wagner/Touadéra. Independent media harassed for Wagner coverage. Very serious.

2019.assessment: APRC. Some media tolerance in peace process context. Wagner disinformation campaign intensifying. Independent journalists covering Wagner face threats. Very serious.

2020.assessment: CPC offensive. Wagner propaganda intensifying. Journalists critical of Wagner expelled or harassed. Several media outlets closed for critical coverage. Very serious.

2021.assessment: Wagner defeated CPC. UN Human Rights reports on Wagner atrocities difficult to report on locally. Journalists covering Wagner face extreme danger. No press freedom on Wagner topics. Very serious overall.

2022.assessment: Wagner/Africa Corps consolidating. France expelled — French-language media hostile. Constitutional referendum — critical coverage impossible. Very serious.

2023.assessment: Wagner → Africa Corps. Constitutional referendum extended term limits — critical coverage dangerous. Some independent radio and print. RSF ~116/180. Very serious.

2024.assessment: Africa Corps. Elections approaching — political coverage restricted. Journalist intimidation continuing. Very serious.

2025.assessment: March 2025: Africa Corps. Elections approaching. Political journalism extremely dangerous. Very serious.

2000.assessment: No statistical capacity data. Patassé era. Very minimal national statistics system. No population census since 1988. Very poor statistical capacity.

sources.citation: World Bank: CAR statistical capacity assessment

2001.assessment: Very poor statistical capacity. Coup attempt disrupted government functions. No census. Very poor.

2002.assessment: Bozizé insurgency disrupted statistical operations. Very poor capacity.

2003.assessment: Bozizé coup. Statistical agency shut down. No statistical system functioning. No statistical system briefly.

2004.assessment: SCI ~16/100. Minimal statistical capacity recovering. Transitional government with donor support beginning to rebuild statistics. Very poor.

2005.assessment: SCI ~18/100. Bozizé election — international support for statistical capacity. ICASEES rebuilding. Very poor.

2006.assessment: SCI ~20/100. HIPC requiring some statistical reporting. Donor projects supporting ICASEES. Very poor.

2007.assessment: SCI ~22/100. Slight improvement. HIPC process. Some census preparatory work. Very poor but recovering.

2008.assessment: SCI ~25/100. Improving statistical capacity. HIPC completion approaching. Some household surveys. Very poor but improving.

2009.assessment: SCI ~28/100. HIPC completion — requirements met. Population census planned (conducted 2003 partially). Some improvements in national accounts. Very poor.

2010.assessment: SCI ~30/100. Best period for CAR statistics. Donor support. ICASEES functional. Some surveys conducted. Poor — approaching very poor/poor boundary.

2011.assessment: SCI ~33/100. Peak pre-crisis. ICASEES operational. Some national accounts published. Population and housing census 2011. Poor.

2012.assessment: SCI ~33/100. Pre-Séléka crisis. Some statistical data produced. Poor but functional.

2013.assessment: Séléka coup March 2013. ICASEES completely non-functional. Staff fled. No statistical operations. GDP estimates for 2013 highly uncertain. No statistical system.

2014.assessment: SCI ~10/100. Civil war continuing. ICASEES partially resuming with Samba-Panza. Minimal statistical capacity. Very poor.

2015.assessment: SCI ~15/100. Bangui Forum. Some reconstruction. ICASEES rebuilding with donor support. Very poor.

2016.assessment: SCI ~20/100. Touadéra elected — international support for statistics. ICASEES partially operational. Very poor.

2017.assessment: SCI ~22/100. Violence continuing but Bangui statistics improving. Very poor.

2018.assessment: SCI ~25/100. Wagner entering creates parallel economy difficult to measure. ICASEES cannot survey Wagner-controlled areas. Very poor.

2019.assessment: SCI ~28/100. APRC — some area recovery improving survey coverage. Very poor.

2020.assessment: SCI ~26/100. CPC offensive. COVID disrupted surveys. Wagner economy unmeasured. Very poor.

2021.assessment: SCI ~28/100 (last year of SCI before discontinuation). Wagner defeated CPC. Territory recovery allowing surveys. ICASEES operational. Very poor.

2022.assessment: WB SCI discontinued 2022. SPI (new indicator) shows very poor. Constitutional referendum. Africa Corps economy partially outside statistical reach. Very poor.

2023.assessment: Very poor statistical capacity. Africa Corps mineral extraction largely unmeasured. ICASEES with limited resources. Very poor.

2024.assessment: Very poor. Elections approaching. Africa Corps. Limited statistical capacity. Very poor.

2025.assessment: March 2025: Very poor statistical capacity. Africa Corps. Elections. Very poor.
